---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Marc Los Huertos"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width = 3, fig.height = 3)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

<table style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
<tr><th></th><th>Independent: Categorical</th><th>Independent: Continuous</th></tr>
<tr><th>Dependent: Continuous</th><td>

### ANOVA / t-test

```{r anova_plot}
set.seed(1)
df <- data.frame(group = rep(c("A","B"), each = 30), y = c(rnorm(30,5), rnorm(30,7)))
ggplot(df, aes(group, y, fill = group)) + geom_boxplot() + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Boxplot (ANOVA)", x = "", y = "")
```

</td><td>

### Linear Regression

```{r lm_plot}
x <- rnorm(60); y <- 2*x + rnorm(60)
df <- data.frame(x,y)
ggplot(df, aes(x,y)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method="lm", se=FALSE) + theme_minimal() + labs(title="Linear Regression")
```

</td></tr>
<tr><th>Dependent: Categorical</th><td>

### Chi-square / Logistic

```{r cat_plot}
df <- data.frame(group = rep(c("A","B"), each=50), outcome = rbinom(100,1,c(0.3,0.6)))
ggplot(df, aes(group,outcome)) + stat_summary(fun=mean, geom="point", size=3) + theme_minimal() + labs(title="Proportion by Group")
```

</td><td>

### Logistic Regression

```{r log_plot}
x <- seq(-3,3,length=100); p <- 1/(1+exp(-x))
df <- data.frame(x,p)
ggplot(df, aes(x,p)) + geom_line() + theme_minimal() + labs(title="Logistic Curve", y="Pr(Y=1)")
```

</td></tr></table>
